what_it_does: This file stores a channel attachment metadata record for the design-assets channel, declaring an image-type attachment that points to a specific image asset and records when it was added.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
There are no code symbols present
standouts: The target uses a custom 'image://' URI scheme with a '(24)' size suffix
It is a minimal, referenceable attachment stub tied to a channel
The timestamp is precise to microseconds
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows a fixed schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Represents the target asset with a custom URI rather than a filesystem path
concerns: The target path contains a space and parentheses that may need escaping or normalization
There is no link to a specific message, thread, or user context
There is no title, alt text, or description for the image asset
notes: A lightweight metadata stub that records an image attachment in the design-assets channel with a custom asset URI and timestamp.
questions: Which message or thread in the channel does this attachment belong to?
What does the '(24)' suffix in the target URI represent—pixel size, version, or something else?
Is the 'image://' URI resolvable by the consuming system?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of recording a channel attachment matches the content; it is a metadata record pointing to an image asset with a timestamp.
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a durable, queryable record of an image attachment associated with a channel, so the asset can be located by id, channel, and target URI.
novelty: The target URI uses a custom 'image://' scheme and includes a dimension-like suffix '(24)'.
contribution: It contributes a concrete attachment identity and asset pointer for an image in the design-assets channel, including a timestamp that can support provenance or correlation in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record with id att-b0c27dbe in the design-assets channel, of type images, pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (24) and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to catalog a specific image asset attachment for the design-assets channel.
